note: All hunter playbooks. Campaign hunters (PORTAL) have a "hunter" field. Generic playbooks have hunter=null. source: full-book | revised-softcover | hardcover-2023 | ssk-2025.

playbooks.name: The Action Scientist
playbooks.description: You may call it the supernatural, but all that means is we don't understand it yet. We'll soon discover what makes it tick. And then I know you'll want to destroy it, but consider how much more we have to learn! I'll keep it in my lab. Perfectly contained.
playbooks.experience_trigger: Whenever you roll and get a total of 6 or less, or when a move tells you to, mark an experience box.
history_questions: You are relations, most likely distant. Tell them what the connection is.
You were academic colleagues at some point in the past. Decide together where and when.
On-again, off-again romance. Decide together if it's off or on right now.
You've been friends ever since you had a Science Adventure a while ago. Decide which evil science nemesis you defeated together.
Just buddies. You get along well when it's time to relax after all the monsters have been dealt with.
They once helped you deal with an 'experiment' that got out of hand. Tell them what it was and let them decide how they helped you sort it out.
They were there when your career changed from scientist to action scientist. Decide together how they helped you out when you fought your first monster.
You used science to get them out of a jam, and they still owe you for it. Ask them what the jam was, then tell them what you did.

moves.name: Test Hypothesis
moves.description: When you test a potential explanation based on data gathered, roll +Sharp. The Keeper will tell you if you're on the right track, missing some critical data, or on the wrong track.

outcomes.10_plus: You can trust that answer.
outcomes.7_9: The Keeper answers honestly, but you wasted time or the testing process destroyed some evidence.
outcomes.miss: The Keeper's answer is unrelated to the facts.

moves.name: Sabotage
moves.description: When you quickly break a complicated gadget, roll +Sharp.

outcomes.10_plus: It's broken. Your choice if it's done quietly or left totally irreparable.
outcomes.7_9: It's broken, but not too hard to fix and you made a bit of a ruckus.
outcomes.miss: The worst thing possible happens—usually explosions.

moves.name: Oblivious to Danger
moves.description: As well as generally not looking out for danger, you are immune to all fear-based moves and powers. You never need to act under pressure to resist fear from any source.

outcomes.general: You are immune to all fear-based moves and powers. You never need to act under pressure to resist fear from any source.

moves.name: The Doors of Perception
moves.description: When you use psychological, pharmacological, or meditation techniques to open your mind to transcendental knowledge, roll +Weird.

outcomes.10_plus: Pick two effects.
outcomes.7_9: Pick one effect, but your mind and perceptions are confused and foggy.
outcomes.miss: You get lost in altered states of consciousness.

choices: Communicate with someone or something that you normally couldn't.
See what is normally invisible: things like physical forces, ghosts and spirits, incursions of other dimensions, and magical effects.
Ask one question from the investigate a mystery list.
Clarity: Get +1 Cool, Sharp, or Weird for the next hour or so (maximum +3).

moves.name: Fieldwork
moves.description: When you put yourself in danger to test a hypothesis, get +1 forward. When you confirm the hypothesis as true or false, mark experience.

outcomes.general: When you put yourself in danger to test a hypothesis, get +1 forward. When you confirm the hypothesis as true or false, mark experience.

gear.note: You have toolkits containing everything you need for your normal science, engineering, and analysis tasks.

options: Lightning gun (3-harm close loud area electricity batteries)
Portable particle accelerator (3-harm close/far messy batteries)

rex_moves_taken.note: List which moves Rex has actually taken here — update after each session when XP is spent

playbooks.luck_special: When you spend a point of Luck, some gadget or machine near you will soon malfunction, break down, or explode (what exactly is up to the Keeper).

area_of_study.note: Pick one focus for your research and training.

options.name: Physics and Cosmology
options.description: You can tell when something is altering the basic laws of reality around you. When you investigate a mystery, you may ask one of these as well as the usual questions: What direction is the source? Is this static or changing? What equipment would tell me more about this?

options.name: Biology and Chemistry
options.description: When you examine a mysterious substance, ask the Keeper what the source is. They'll tell you if it's biological or not, and if the source is mundane, supernatural, extraterrestrial, or extra-dimensional.

options.name: Neurology and Psychology
options.description: When you're talking to someone and assessing their motives, roll +Sharp. On a 10+, the Keeper will tell you if they're really human and whether they're an ally, bystander, villain, minion, or monster. On a 7–9, the Keeper will tell you if there's something off about them but you're not sure what it is yet. On a miss, you reveal too much about yourself instead.

options.name: Computers and Electronics
options.description: When you access a secure computer system or electronic device, or change what it does, roll +Cool. On a 10+, you get access, or change it (within the limits of the system). On a 7–9, either it takes extra time or something unexpected happens. On a miss, it all goes wrong in the worst way possible.

options.name: Violence
options.description: You may roll +Sharp to kick some ass instead of +Tough. When you make a scientific attack, either do +1 harm or take +1 forward.

options.name: Mechanics and Engineering
options.description: When you fix machinery, it takes much less time than expected. You also don't need all the proper tools to work on machines. You may also combine any two devices, rebuilding them together. The new gadget has all the abilities of each component, and is the size of the larger one. It has all the tags of both components. You may add, delete, or change one tag.

options.name: Space
options.description: You have +1 ongoing with rocketry or astronaut stuff. You have access to a space launch facility (including use of spacecraft).

playbooks.moves_note: Pick 2 from 5 moves. Confirmed complete against SSK source — the playbook has exactly 5 optional moves (no missing entries). Rex uses Weird Science as his Weird move (recommended in book).

playbooks.name: The Sidekick
playbooks.description: I don't know much about the occult or monsters, or this whole deal. But I found a Hero. Someone better than me. They need a hand now and then, to do the heroic stuff. Someday, maybe, I can be a big damn hero too.

stats.note: Pick one line. Also take +1 on your hero's best rating (ask their player which is highest).

playbooks.experience_trigger: Whenever you roll and get a total of 6 or less, or when a move tells you to, mark an experience box.
history_questions: (For the hero) This hunter is your hero.
This hunter makes you a bit jealous of their relationship with your hero.
This hunter has been your friend for many years. Where did you meet?
This hunter bonded with you after defeating a monster together, the first time you worked as a team. What was it?
This hunter knew you back when you were a nobody. Tell them something about the person you used to be, something you're now ashamed of.
This hunter introduced you to your hero.
This hunter saved your life when you were distracted, so you owe them big time.
This hunter's pretty great, they could have almost been your hero. Tell them why they're not quite as good.

moves.name: There's no "I" in "team"
moves.description: You get +1 on all help out rolls, or +2 if it's for your hero.

outcomes.general: You get +1 on all help out rolls, or +2 if it's for your hero.

moves.name: Caddy
moves.description: You carry all your hero's stuff around. That means you can use anything from their gear list, unless they are currently holding it. If it's something that there would be a bunch of, or some spares, you can use it even if they are.

outcomes.general: You can use anything from your hero's gear list, unless they are currently holding it. If it's something that there would be a bunch of, or some spares, you can use it even if they are.

moves.name: As You Wish
moves.description: Mark experience whenever your hero orders or requests that you do something you don't want to do, and you go ahead and do it anyway.

outcomes.general: Mark experience whenever your hero orders or requests that you do something you don't want to do, and you go ahead and do it anyway.

moves.name: Eek!
moves.description: When a monster (or anyone really) appears on the scene and you find a hiding spot, roll +Sharp.

outcomes.10_plus: You hide in the best nearby spot before the monster sees you.
outcomes.7_9: Pick one: you hide okay, but the monster is now between you and escape; you hide okay for now, but your hiding spot is bound to be spotted soon; you hide okay, but leave something important out in the open.
outcomes.miss: You fail to hide in time.

moves.name: Bodyguard
moves.description: If something bad is going to happen to your hero, your comrades, or a bystander, and you are right there, you may throw yourself in harm's way. Whatever was going to happen to them happens to you instead, and you mark experience.

outcomes.general: You may throw yourself in harm's way. Whatever was going to happen to them happens to you instead, and you mark experience.

moves.name: Me Too!
moves.description: If your hero makes a roll for a move, and you copy what they did, making the same move, you may choose not to roll. Instead, use your hero's die roll (but adjusted with your own rating).

outcomes.general: You may choose not to roll. Instead, use your hero's die roll, but adjusted with your own rating.

moves.name: I Can Make You Look Good
moves.description: If you are helping out your hero, you may swap one or both your dice with theirs, as long as you come out with the lowest total. Calculate your results as if you had each rolled the dice you now have in front of you.

outcomes.general: You may swap one or both your dice with your hero's, as long as you come out with the lowest total. Calculate your results as if you had each rolled the dice you now have in front of you.

moves.name: Apprentice
moves.description: Take a move that your hero has.

outcomes.general: Take a move that your hero has.

hero_item.note: Pick one item from your hero's gear options.

playbooks.name: The Changeling
playbooks.description: Brought up as human, but not. Struggles with their two natures — neither world fully claims them.
playbooks.experience_trigger: Whenever you roll and get a total of 6 or less, or when a move tells you to, mark an experience box.
history_questions: They discovered you aren't human. Ask them how they realised and how (if?) they told you.
You suspect they know more about your heritage than they're telling. Ask if your suspicion is correct.
They tried to slay you, but decided you were innocent—or at least not evil. Ask what convinced them.
You see them as your anchor to humanity. Ask them what sort of example they set.
You are romantically involved with them, or a close relative of theirs. Decide together the exact relationship.
They are family in every way but blood. Decide together the details of your relationship.
They claim you and your people are part of their destiny. Ask them what they told you and tell them your reaction to this.
They see you as a protégé to train for hunting. Ask them how persistent they are in this agenda.

origin.note: The Changeling has no fixed fey-type origin choice in the SSK edition. Heritage is defined through the Unknown Heritage tag system (see unknown_heritage field) and move picks. Alan's specific heritage is an open campaign question.

moves.name: Glamour
moves.description: Your appearance is clearly inhuman, making regular interaction with bystanders difficult. However, when feeling calm, you can maintain an illusion of humanity. You may drop your glamour and display your supernatural power to turn any roll into a 10. Your unknown heritage stirs up trouble right away or soon after.

outcomes.general: When feeling calm, you can maintain an illusion of humanity. You may drop your glamour and display your supernatural power to turn any roll into a 10. Your unknown heritage stirs up trouble right away or soon after.

moves.name: Inhuman Talent
moves.description: You're naturally skilled with a specific type of magic—perhaps plants, ice, illusions, or summer. You have +1 ongoing when you use a basic Weird move that uses these powers. In addition, at the start of each session, hold 2. Spend your hold for +1 forward on a move your powers can enhance.

outcomes.general: +1 ongoing when you use a basic Weird move that uses your specific powers. At the start of each session, hold 2. Spend your hold for +1 forward on a move your powers can enhance.

moves.name: They Are My People
moves.description: Once per session, when you protect someone, act as if you rolled a 12. You gain +1 forward when acting under pressure to resist instincts like your unknown heritage, other dark urges, and 'fight or flight' situations.

outcomes.general: Once per session, when you protect someone, act as if you rolled a 12. You gain +1 forward when acting under pressure to resist instincts like your unknown heritage, other dark urges, and 'fight or flight' situations.

moves.name: Force of Nature
moves.description: You have a supernatural attack from your heritage. Choose its type: lightning, insect swarms, magical blades, or something else. When you unleash your power, roll +Weird.

outcomes.10_plus: You summon a 1-harm close weapon for a short time. Pick two extras.
outcomes.7_9: You summon a 1-harm close weapon for a short time. Pick one extra.
outcomes.miss: The power turns against you.

moves.choices_note: Extras: +1 harm, hand, far, autonomous, distracting, ignore-armour, area, grab, blind, stun

moves.name: Lockdown
moves.description: You can stun people with a power like a petrifying gaze, overwhelming illusions, or bewildering moths. When you freeze someone, roll +Weird.

outcomes.10_plus: The target is immobilised for a few minutes or until something snaps them out of it.
outcomes.7_9: The target is immobilised for as long as you focus your attention, or until something snaps them out of it.
outcomes.miss: It causes a magical backlash.

moves.name: Faerie Gossip
moves.description: You can communicate with local spirits and mystical creatures. When you investigate a mystery by questioning these beings, you may roll +Weird instead of +Sharp, and may ask the following questions as well as the usual ones: Is anything affecting the supernatural environment? How long has __________ been in the area? When did __________ first arrive?

outcomes.10_plus: Hold 2 (as investigate a mystery). May also ask: Is anything affecting the supernatural environment? How long has __________ been in the area? When did __________ first arrive?
outcomes.7_9: Hold 1 (as investigate a mystery). May also ask the additional questions.
outcomes.miss: As normal miss for investigate a mystery.

moves.name: Renewal
moves.description: You can speed the healing of those around you. When teammates and allies in your presence recover harm by any means, they recover 1 extra harm.

outcomes.general: When teammates and allies in your presence recover harm by any means, they recover 1 extra harm.

moves.name: Strange Paths
moves.description: You can walk (or drive) between worlds. However, you don't know the way very well. When you take an otherworld shortcut, roll +Weird.

outcomes.10_plus: You arrive quicker than otherwise possible.
outcomes.7_9: You find trouble along the way. Perhaps you get only partway, encounter locals, or only save a little time.
outcomes.miss: You arrive a little too late or have to deal with something blocking the way.

options: Fairly new car in decent condition
Pocket knife or multitool (1-harm hand useful small)
Talisman or artifact (1-harm close magic ignore-armour)
A smartphone, mobile game device, or music player

playbooks.name: The Monstrous
playbooks.description: I feel the hunger, the lust to destroy. But I fight it: I never give in. I'm not human any more, not really, but I have to protect those who still are. That way I can tell myself I'm different to the other monsters. Sometimes I can even believe it.

stats.note: The Monstrous always gets +3 Weird. Pick one line:

playbooks.experience_trigger: Whenever you roll and get a total of 6 or less, or when a move tells you to, mark an experience box.
history_questions: You lost control one time, and almost killed them. Ask them how they stopped you.
They tried to slay you, but you proved you're on the side of good. Ask them what convinced them.
You are romantically obsessed with them. Ask them if they know, and if they reciprocate.
Close relations, or a distant descendant. Tell them which.
You saved them from another of your kind, and prevented reprisals against that individual creature (maybe it's another good one, or maybe it has a hold over you).
They are tied to your curse or origin. Tell them how.
You fought together against the odds, and prevailed.
They saved you from another hunter who was prepared to kill you. Ask them what happened.

curse.note: Pick one curse to define your breed.

options.name: Feed
options.description: You must subsist on living humans—it might take the form of blood, brains, or spiritual essence but it must be from people. You need to act under pressure to resist feeding whenever a perfect opportunity presents itself.

options.name: Vulnerability
options.description: Pick a substance. You suffer +1 harm when you suffer harm from it. If you are bound or surrounded by it, you must act under pressure to use your powers.

options.name: Pure Drive
options.description: One emotion rules you. Pick from: hunger, hate, anger, fear, jealousy, greed, joy, pride, envy, lust, or cruelty. Whenever you have a chance to indulge that emotion, you must do so immediately, or act under pressure to resist.

options.name: Dark Master
options.description: You have an evil lord who doesn't know you changed sides. They still give you orders, and they do not tolerate refusal. Or failure.

outcomes.10_plus: Hold 3. You may spend your hold to give them an order. Regular people will follow your order, whatever it is. Hunters can choose whether they do it or not — if they do, they mark experience.

moves.name: Unquenchable Vitality
moves.description: When you have taken harm, you can heal yourself. Roll +Cool.

outcomes.10_plus: Heal 2-harm and stabilise your injuries.
outcomes.7_9: Heal 1-harm and stabilise your injuries.

playbooks.name: The Flake
playbooks.description: Everything's connected. But not everyone can see the patterns, and most people don't even look that hard. But me, I can never stop looking deeper. I can never stop seeing the truth. I spot the patterns. That's how I found the monsters, and that's how I help kill them.
playbooks.luck_special: When you spend a point of Luck, pick an aspect of the current situation. The Keeper will tell you what other conspiracies that aspect connects to.
playbooks.experience_trigger: Whenever you roll and get a total of 6 or less, or when a move tells you to, mark an experience box.
history_questions: They're somehow tied into it all. You've been keeping an eye on them.
They're a close relative. Ask them to decide exactly what.
Old friends, who originally met through a long chain of coincidences.
You went through hell together: maybe a monster, maybe military service, maybe time in an institution. Whatever it was, it bound you together, and you have total trust in each other.
Members of the same support group.
Fellow freaks.
The signs all pointed to working together. So you found them and now you work together.
You know each other through cryptozoology and conspiracy theory websites.
playbooks.moves_note: Pick three Flake moves to start. All eight are available at character creation.

moves.name: Connect the Dots
moves.description: At the beginning of each mystery, if you look for the wider patterns that current events might be part of, roll +Sharp.

outcomes.10_plus: Hold 3.
outcomes.7_9: Hold 1.
outcomes.miss: No hold.

moves.choices_note: Spend your hold during the mystery to ask the Keeper any one of the following questions:
choices: Is this person connected to current events more than they are saying?
When and where will the next critical event occur?
What does the monster want from this person?
Is this connected to previous mysteries we have investigated?
How does this mystery connect to the bigger picture?

Retire this hunter to safety.
Get back one used Luck point.

playbooks.name: The Chosen
playbooks.description: Your birth was prophesied. You are the Chosen One, and with your abilities you can save the world. If you fail, all will be destroyed. It all rests on you. Only you.

stats.note: The Chosen may have two ratings at +2.

playbooks.experience_trigger: Whenever you roll and get a total of 6 or less, or when a move tells you to, mark an experience box.

fate.note: Pick how you found out about your fate, then pick two heroic and two doom tags. Whenever you mark off a point of Luck, the Keeper will throw something from your fate at you.

special_weapon.note: Design your weapon by choosing a form, three business-end options, and a material.

history_questions: You are close blood relations. Ask them exactly how close.
They are destined to be your mentor. Tell them how this was revealed.
Your best friend in the world, who you trust totally.
A rival at first, but you came to a working arrangement.
Romantic entanglement, or fated to be romantically entangled.
Just friends, from school or work or something. Ask them what.
They could have been the Chosen One instead of you, but they failed some trial. Tell them how they failed.
You saved their life, back when they didn't know monsters were real. Tell them what you saved them from.

moves.name: Destiny's Plaything
moves.description: At the beginning of each mystery, roll +Weird to see what is revealed about your immediate future.

outcomes.10_plus: The Keeper will reveal a useful detail about the coming mystery.
outcomes.7_9: You get a vague hint about it.
outcomes.miss: Something bad is going to happen to you.

moves.name: I'm Here For A Reason
moves.description: There's something you are destined to do. Work out the details with the Keeper, based on your fate. You cannot die until it comes to pass. If you die in play, you must spend a Luck point and will somehow recover or be returned to life. Once your task is done (or you use up all your Luck), all bets are off.

outcomes.general: Passive — you cannot die before your destiny is fulfilled. Spend Luck to survive death.

moves.name: The Big Entrance
moves.description: When you make a showy entrance into a dangerous situation, roll +Cool.

outcomes.10_plus: Everyone stops to watch and listen until you finish your opening speech.
outcomes.7_9: You pick one person or monster to stop, watch and listen until you finish talking.
outcomes.miss: You're marked as the biggest threat by all enemies who are present.

moves.name: Devastating
moves.description: When you inflict harm, you may inflict +1 harm.

outcomes.general: Passive — inflict +1 harm whenever you deal harm.

moves.name: Dutiful
moves.description: When your fate rears its ugly head and you act in accordance with any of your fate tags (either heroic or doom), mark experience. If it's a heroic tag, take +1 forward.

outcomes.general: Passive — mark experience when acting on fate tags; +1 forward if heroic.

moves.name: Invincible
moves.description: You always count as having 2-armour. This doesn't stack with other protection.

outcomes.general: Passive — you always have 2-armour (does not stack).

moves.name: Resilience
moves.description: You heal faster than normal people. Any time your harm gets healed, heal an extra point. Additionally, your wounds count as 1-harm less for the purpose of the Keeper's harm moves.

outcomes.general: Passive — heal +1 extra whenever healed; wounds count as 1-harm less for harm moves.

playbooks.moves_note: You get Destiny's Plaything and I'm Here For A Reason automatically. Pick one more from the remaining five.

gear.note: You can have protective gear worth 1-armour if you want it. You also have a special weapon you are destined to wield — see the special_weapon field.
items: Special weapon (designed at creation — form + 3 business-end options + material)

playbooks.luck_special: When you spend a point of Luck, the Keeper will bring your fate into play.
improvements: Take a move from another playbook
Take a move from another playbook
advanced_improvements: Get +1 to any rating, max +3
Get back one used Luck point
Change this hunter to a new type
Create a second hunter to play as well as this one
Mark two of the basic moves as advanced
Mark another two of the basic moves as advanced
Retire this hunter to safety
Delete one of your Doom tags, and (optionally) one of your Heroic tags. You have changed that aspect of your destiny.

playbooks.name: The Crooked
playbooks.description: Yeah, I've been around the block. A bit of this, a bit of that. When I came across the secret underworld of monsters and magic… well… it wasn't so different from the underworld I already knew. It was easy to find an angle, just like before.
playbooks.experience_trigger: Whenever you roll a six or less, or when a move tells you to, mark an experience box.

background.note: Pick one background. This determines an extra ability you have from your criminal past.

options.name: Hoodlum
options.description: You can use Tough instead of Charm to manipulate someone with threats of violence.

options.name: Burglar
options.description: When you break into a secure location, roll +Sharp. On 10+ pick three, on 7-9 pick two: you get in undetected, you get out undetected, you don't leave a mess, you find what you were after.

options.name: Grifter
options.description: When you are about to manipulate someone, you can ask the Keeper 'What will convince this person to do what I want?' The Keeper must answer honestly, but not necessarily completely.

options.name: Fixer
options.description: If you need to buy, sell, or hire, roll +Charm. 10+: you know just the person. 7-9: you know the only person, but there's a complication (pick one: you owe them; they screwed you over; you screwed them over). Miss: the only person who can help absolutely hates you.

options.name: Assassin
options.description: When you take your first shot at an unsuspecting target, do +2 Harm.

options.name: Charlatan
options.description: When you want people to think you are using magic, roll +Cool. 10+: audience amazed and fooled. 7-9: tripped up a couple times. You may also manipulate people with fortune telling — ask 'What are they hoping for right now?' as a free question (even on a miss).

options.name: Pickpocket
options.description: When you steal something small, roll +Charm. 10+: you get it and they didn't notice. 7-9: either you don't grab it, you grab the wrong thing, or they remember you later.

heat.note: Pick at least two heat entries and name the people involved.

underworld.note: Pick how you discovered the real underworld. Options include: vampire/werewolf/troll/reptiloid target; worked with sorcerer/demon/faerie/psychic; hired by immortal/god/outsider/witch; ran into goblins/ghouls/dream-eater/salamander.

history_questions: This hunter knows about your criminal past. Tell them what crimes they saw you commit.
This hunter was there when you decided to give up the life and hunt monsters instead. Work out together what happened.
This hunter is your younger sibling or child (possibly adopted). You look out for them.
This hunter is a cousin or more distant relative.
This hunter saved your life when a monster had the drop on you. Now you owe them one.
This hunter worked with you on a semi-legal or illegal job. Work out what it was.
This hunter is your moral compass. When you talk over things with them, their advice keeps you on the straight and narrow.
You're powerfully attracted to this hunter. Maybe someday you'll deserve them.

moves.name: Artifact
moves.description: You 'found' a magical artifact with handy powers, and kept it. Pick one: Protective amulet (1-armour magic recharge), Lucky charm (may be used as a Luck point, once only), Grimoire (studying the book gives +1 forward to use magic), Skeleton key (opens any magically sealed lock), Imp stone (a weak demon is bound to serve the holder; the imp must be summoned with the use magic move).

outcomes.general: Passive — you possess a chosen magical artifact.

moves.name: Crew
moves.description: You have a regular crew, a team of three or four people who will help you out with pretty much anything. They count as a team.

outcomes.general: Passive — you have a team of 3-4 allies.

moves.name: Deal with the Devil
moves.description: You sold your soul to the Devil. Pick one or two things you got out of the deal: wealth, fame, youth, sensual gratification, skill (add +1 to two ratings). Payment is due when you die, in six months (if you picked two things), or otherwise in a year.

outcomes.general: Passive — you have a devil's deal with benefits and a deferred cost.

moves.name: Friends on the Force
moves.description: You know a few cops who can be persuaded to look the other way, or do you a favour, for certain considerations. You can act under pressure to get in touch with them when you need to divert law enforcement attention. There will be a cost, although maybe not right now.

outcomes.general: Passive — you can call in law enforcement favours via act under pressure.

moves.name: Made
moves.description: You're 'made' in a gang. Name the gang and describe how their operations tie into your background. You can call on gang members to help you out, but they'll expect to be paid. Your bosses will have requests for you now and again, but you'll be paid. Minor trouble will be overlooked, but you better not screw over any other made gangsters.

outcomes.general: Passive — gang membership grants muscle and pay, but comes with obligations.

moves.name: Driver
moves.description: You have +1 ongoing while driving, plus you can hotwire anything (the older it is, the fewer tools you need). You also own two handy, widely-available vehicles (perhaps a sportscar and a van).

outcomes.general: Passive — +1 ongoing while driving; can hotwire vehicles; own two vehicles.

moves.name: Home Ground
moves.description: Your crew made a point of keeping the locals happy — keeping them safe, ensuring things always went down okay. When you're back in your old neighbourhood, you can always find people who will hide you or help you with a minor favour, no questions asked.

outcomes.general: Passive — your old neighbourhood gives you shelter and minor favours.

moves.name: Notorious
moves.description: You have a reputation from your criminal past. When you reveal who you are, your terrifying reputation counts as a reason for people to do what you ask, for the manipulate someone move. Revealing your identity can create other problems later, of course.

outcomes.general: Passive — your reputation qualifies as leverage for manipulate someone.

playbooks.moves_note: Pick two Crooked moves to start.
playbooks.luck_special: Whenever you spend a Luck point, someone from your past (heat or underworld) will re-appear in your life. Soon.
improvements: Gain an ally: one of your old crew
Recover a stash of money from the old days, enough to live without care... for a year or two.
Take a move from another playbook
Take a move from another playbook
advanced_improvements: Get +1 to any rating, max +3
Change this hunter to a new type
Create a second hunter to play as well as this one
Mark two of the basic moves as advanced
Mark another two of the basic moves as advanced
Retire this hunter to safety
Erase one used luck mark from your playbook.

playbooks.name: The Divine
playbooks.description: I am the Light, the Sword. I am sent to defend the meek from Darkness. All Evil fears me, for I am its end.
playbooks.experience_trigger: Whenever your roll totals 6 or less, or when a move tells you to, mark an experience box.

mission.note: Pick one mission that defines why you are on Earth.
options: You are here to fight the schemes of an Adversary.
The End of Days approaches. Your role is to guide these hunters and prevent it from coming to pass.
The End of Days approaches. Your role is to guide these hunters and ensure it comes to pass.
You have been exiled. You must work for the cause of Good without drawing attention from your brothers and sisters, as they are bound to execute you for your crimes.
One of the other hunters has a crucial role to play in events to come. You must prepare them for their role, and protect them at any cost.

history_questions: If you are protecting another hunter as your mission, tell them: You have a crucial role in what is to come. I am here to guide and defend you.
They should not be involved in this situation: the prophecies didn't mention them at all. This gets your attention but you don't know what it means yet.
They are, at heart, a good and righteous person. You must help them stay that way.
They are an abomination, and should be destroyed. Except you can't — work out with them why not.
Their prayer (perhaps an informal or even unconscious prayer) summoned you.
They fill you with feelings of sexual infatuation. You are confused by the associated mortal emotions.
They saved your life, and you understand (intellectually at least) that you owe them for it.
They're the person you go to for advice on mortal stuff (e.g. sex, food, drugs, television, etc).

moves.name: Boss from Beyond
moves.description: At the beginning of each mystery, roll +Weird. On 10+, your Superiors ask you to do something simple. On 7-9, they ask you to do something complicated or difficult. In either case, you get to ask them one investigate-a-mystery question right now. On a miss, you are required to do something terrible. If you don't accomplish what they've ordered, you cannot use this move again until you have made up for your failure.

outcomes.10_plus: Superiors ask something simple; ask them one investigate-a-mystery question right now.
outcomes.7_9: Superiors ask something complicated or difficult; ask them one question.
outcomes.miss: You are required to do something terrible; move is locked until you atone.

moves.name: Angel Wings
moves.description: You can go instantly to anywhere you've visited before, or to a person you know well. When you carry one or two people with you, roll +Weird.

outcomes.10_plus: You all go where you wanted.
outcomes.7_9: You don't quite manage it — either you are all separated, or you all appear in the wrong place.
outcomes.miss: Something goes wrong with the teleportation.

moves.name: What I Need, When I Need It
moves.description: You may store any small object you own, putting it into a magical space nobody can get to. You may retrieve anything you stored at any time; it appears in your hand.

outcomes.general: Passive — store and retrieve small objects from a personal magical space.

moves.name: Soothe
moves.description: When you talk to someone for a few seconds in a quiet voice, you can calm them down, blocking any panic, anger, or other negative emotions they have. This works even if the thing that freaked them out is still present, as long as your voice can be heard.

outcomes.general: Passive — your voice suppresses panic and negative emotions in proximity.

moves.name: Lay On Hands
moves.description: Your touch can heal injury and disease. When you lay your hands on someone hurt, roll +Cool.

outcomes.10_plus: Heal 2 harm or an illness, plus they're stabilized.
outcomes.7_9: Heal the harm or illness as on a 10+, but you take it into yourself.
outcomes.miss: Your aura causes them extra harm.

moves.name: Cast Out Evil
moves.description: You may banish an unnatural creature from your presence. Roll +Tough. The banished creature is unharmed and you have no control over where it goes. This move may be used on unnatural hunters (e.g. the Monstrous).

outcomes.10_plus: It is banished.
outcomes.7_9: It takes a little while for the banishing to take effect — the creature has time to make one or two actions.
outcomes.miss: Something is keeping it here. That's bad.

moves.name: Smite
moves.description: Your body and divine weapon always count as a weakness against the monsters you fight. Your unarmed attacks are 2-harm intimate hand messy.

outcomes.general: Passive — your body and divine weapon are always a weakness; unarmed attacks are 2-harm intimate hand messy.

playbooks.moves_note: Pick three Divine moves to start.

gear.note: Pick one divine weapon and take divine armour (1-armour holy).
divine_weapons: Razor whip (3-harm hand area messy holy)
Five demon bag (3-harm close magic holy)

playbooks.luck_special: When you spend a Luck, you get word your Mission requires something difficult that must be done. By you. Urgently.
improvements: Gain a lesser divine being as an ally, sent from above to help with your mission
Take a move from another playbook
Take a move from another playbook

Erase one used Luck mark from your playbook.
Change your mission. Select a different mission from the normal options, or (with the Keeper's agreement) a new mission of your creation.

playbooks.name: The Expert
playbooks.description: I have dedicated my life to the study of the unnatural. I know their habits, their weaknesses. I may not be youngest or strongest, but my knowledge makes me the biggest threat.
playbooks.experience_trigger: Whenever you roll and get a total of 6 or less, or when a move tells you to, mark an experience box.

haven.note: Pick three haven options. The haven is your safe place to work.

options.name: Lore Library
options.description: When you hit the books, take +1 forward to investigate the mystery (where historical or reference works are appropriate).

options.name: Mystical Library
options.description: Preparing with your occult tomes and grimoires, take +1 forward for use magic.

options.name: Protection Spells
options.description: Your haven is safe from monsters — they cannot enter. Monsters might be able to do something special to evade the wards, but not easily.

options.name: Armory
options.description: You have a stockpile of mystical and rare monster-killing weapons and items. If you need a special weapon, roll +Weird. 10+: you have it (and plenty). 7-9: you have it, but only the minimum. Miss: you've got the wrong thing.

options.name: Infirmary
options.description: You can heal people, and have space for one or two to recuperate. The Keeper will tell you how long recovery is likely to take, and if you need extra supplies or help.

options.name: Workshop
options.description: You have a space for building and repairing guns, cars and other gadgets. Work out with the Keeper how long any repair or construction will take, and if you need extra supplies or help.

options.name: Oubliette
options.description: This room is isolated from every kind of monster, spirit and magic that you know about. Anything stashed in there can't be found, can't do any magic, and can't get out.

options.name: Panic Room
options.description: This has essential supplies and is protected by normal and mystical means. You can hide out there for a few days, safe from pretty much anything.

options.name: Magical Laboratory
options.description: You have a mystical lab with all kinds of weird ingredients and tools useful for casting spells (like the use magic move, big magic, and any other magical moves).

history_questions: They are your student, apprentice, ward, or child. Between you, decide which.
They came to you for advice, and your advice got them out of trouble. Ask them what the trouble was.
They know about some of your dark secrets, but they've agreed to keep quiet about them. Tell them what they know.
A distant relation. Tell them exactly what.
You were previously both members of an eldritch group, now disbanded. Ask them why they left, then tell them why you did.
They once helped you get a singular item that is now part of your haven. Tell them what it was.
You were taught by the same master. Ask them how it ended.
You saved their life in a tight spot. Tell them what happened.

moves.name: I've Read About This Sort Of Thing
moves.description: Roll +Sharp instead of +Cool when you act under pressure.

outcomes.general: Passive — substitute +Sharp for +Cool on act under pressure.

moves.name: Often Right
moves.description: When a hunter comes to you for advice about a problem, give them your honest opinion and advice. If they take your advice, they get +1 ongoing while following your advice, and you mark experience.

outcomes.general: Passive — honest advice grants +1 ongoing to the recipient and marks your XP.

moves.name: Preparedness
moves.description: When you need something unusual or rare, roll +Sharp.

outcomes.10_plus: You have it here right now.
outcomes.7_9: You have it, but not here: it will take some time to get it.
outcomes.miss: You know where it is, but it's somewhere real bad.

moves.name: It Wasn't As Bad As It Looked
moves.description: Once per mystery, you may attempt to keep going despite your injuries. Roll +Cool.

outcomes.10_plus: Heal 2 harm and stabilize your wounds.
outcomes.7_9: You may either stabilize or heal 1 harm.
outcomes.miss: It was worse than it looked: the Keeper may inflict a harm move on you, or make your wounds unstable.

moves.name: Precise Strike
moves.description: When you inflict harm on a monster, you can aim for a weak spot. Roll +Tough.

outcomes.10_plus: You inflict +2 harm.
outcomes.7_9: You inflict +1 harm.
outcomes.miss: You leave yourself open for the monster to hit you.

moves.name: The Woman (or Man) With The Plan
moves.description: At the beginning of each mystery, roll +Sharp.

outcomes.10_plus: Hold 2.
outcomes.7_9: Hold 1.
outcomes.miss: The Keeper holds 1 they can spend to have you be in the worst place, unprepared and unready.

choices: Spend hold to be where you need to be, prepared and ready.
moves.choices_note: Spend hold one-for-one to be in exactly the right place, prepared.

moves.name: Dark Past
moves.description: You dabbled in the worst sort of mystical arts before you became one of the good guys. If you trawl through your memories for something relevant to the case at hand, roll +Weird.

outcomes.10_plus: Ask the Keeper two questions from the list.
outcomes.7_9: Ask one question.
outcomes.miss: You can ask a question anyway but that will mean you were personally complicit in creating the situation you are now dealing with.

choices: When I dealt with this creature (or one of its kind), what did I learn?
What black magic do I know that could help here?
Do I know anyone who might be behind this?
Who do I know who can help us right now?
moves.choices_note: Questions to ask the Keeper when you spend results.

playbooks.moves_note: Pick two Expert moves to start.

items: Mallet & wooden stakes (3-harm intimate slow wooden)
Cold iron sword (2-harm hand messy iron)

playbooks.luck_special: When you spend a Luck, you'll discover something happening now is related to something you were involved in years ago. Maybe it's directly due to your actions, or maybe someone else is dabbling in the same area.
improvements: Add an option to your haven
Add an option to your haven
Take a move from another playbook
Take a move from another playbook

Get back one used Luck point
Change this hunter to a new type
Create a second hunter to play as well as this one
Mark two of the basic moves as advanced
Mark another two of the basic moves as advanced
Retire this hunter to safety.

playbooks.name: The Initiate
playbooks.description: Since the dawn of history, we have been the bulwark against Darkness. We know the Evils of the world, and we stand against them so that the mass of humanity need not fear. We are the Flame that cleanses the Shadows.
playbooks.experience_trigger: Whenever you roll and get a total of 6 or less, or when a move tells you to, mark an experience box.

sect.note: Define your ancient secret order. Pick two good traditions and one bad tradition.

history_questions: They are a lay member of your Sect.
You fought together when the tide of monsters seemed unstoppable. Ask them how it went.
Friends, but they first met you under your cover identity, and learned about the Sect later. Ask how they feel about that.
They are your close relative, or partner/spouse. Decide between you exactly what the relationship is.
Fellow ancient weapons/martial arts club members.
They're described in the prophecies, but the role they will play isn't stated.
An ex-member of the Sect, but still friends. Ask them why they left or got thrown out.
You met researching mystical weirdness, and you've been occult comrades ever since.

moves.name: The Sect (Mandatory)
moves.description: When you are in good standing with your Sect, at the beginning of each mystery, roll +Charm. On 10+, they provide some useful info or help in the field. On 7-9, you get a mission associated with the mystery, and if you do it you'll get some info or help too. On a miss, they ask you to do something bad. If you fail a mission or refuse an order, you'll be in trouble with the Sect until you atone.

outcomes.10_plus: Sect provides useful info or field support.
outcomes.7_9: You get a mission; completing it earns info or support.
outcomes.miss: They ask you to do something bad.

moves.name: Ancient Fighting Arts
moves.description: When using an old-fashioned hand weapon, you inflict +1 harm and get +1 whenever you roll protect someone.

outcomes.general: Passive — +1 harm with old-fashioned hand weapons; +1 on protect someone.

moves.name: Mystic
moves.description: Every time you successfully use magic, take +1 forward.

outcomes.general: Passive — +1 forward after every successful use magic.

moves.name: Fortunes
moves.description: The Sect has ancient prophecies or divination techniques to predict the future. Once per mystery, you may use them. Roll +Weird.

outcomes.10_plus: Hold 3.
outcomes.7_9: Hold 1.
outcomes.miss: You get bad information and the Keeper decides how that affects you.

choices: Have a useful object ready.
Be somewhere you are needed, just in time.
Take +1 forward, or give +1 forward to another hunter.
Retroactively warn someone about an attack, so that it doesn't happen.
moves.choices_note: Spend hold one-for-one on these effects.

moves.name: Sacred Oath
moves.description: You may bind yourself to a single goal, forsaking something during your quest. Get the Keeper's agreement on this — it should match the goal in importance and difficulty. While you keep your oath and work towards your goal, mark experience at the end of every session and get +1 on any rolls that directly help achieve the goal. If you break the oath, take -1 ongoing until you have atoned.

outcomes.general: Passive — while keeping an oath: XP at session end + +1 on direct rolls. Break it: -1 ongoing until atoned.

moves.name: Mentor
moves.description: You have a mentor in the Sect: name them. When you contact your mentor for info, roll +Sharp.

outcomes.10_plus: You get an answer to your question, no problem.
outcomes.7_9: They're either busy and can't help, or they answer the question but you owe a favour.
outcomes.miss: Your question causes trouble.

moves.name: Apprentice
moves.description: You have an apprentice: name them. Your job is to teach them the Sect's ways. They count as an ally: subordinate (motivation: to follow your instructions to the letter).

outcomes.general: Passive — you have a named apprentice ally.

moves.name: Helping Hand
moves.description: When you successfully help out another hunter, they get +2 instead of the usual +1.

outcomes.general: Passive — your help out gives +2 instead of +1.

moves.name: That Old Black Magic
moves.description: When you use magic, you can ask a question from the investigate a mystery move as your effect.

outcomes.general: Passive — use magic can yield an investigate-a-mystery question as its effect.

playbooks.moves_note: The Sect move is mandatory. Pick three more from the remaining list.

gear.note: If the Sect has fighting arts or obsolete gear, pick three old-fashioned weapons. If the Sect has modernised or nifty gadgets, pick two modern weapons. Otherwise, pick two old-fashioned and one modern. You also get old-fashioned armour (1-armour heavy).
old_fashioned_weapons: Big axe (3-harm hand messy slow heavy)

playbooks.luck_special: When you spend a Luck, something goes wrong for your Sect: an ill-advised project or a disastrous operation. It might be your fault; it could be due to the Sect's bad traditions.
improvements: Get command of your chapter of the Sect
Get a Sect team under your command

Take a move from another playbook

Change this hunter to a new type

Mark two of the basic moves as advanced
Mark another two of the basic moves as advanced
Retire this hunter to safety
Become the leader, or effective leader, of the whole Sect.
Get back one used Luck point.

playbooks.name: The Mundane
playbooks.description: You heard about how monsters only pick on people with crazy powers who can fight back on even terms? Yeah, me neither. But, hell, I ended up in this monster-hunting team so I gotta do what I can, right?
playbooks.experience_trigger: Whenever you roll and get a total of 6 or less, or when a move tells you to, mark an experience box.
history_questions: You are close relations. Tell them exactly how you're related.
Initially rivals, you both now respect each others' talents.
Romantically involved, or you just have a crush on them. Ask them which they prefer.
They're your hero, exactly the kind of monster hunter you aspire to be. Tell them why you worship them.
Good friends. Tell them if it's from way back, or recently.
You're a bit suspicious of them (maybe due to their unnatural powers or something like that).
They introduced you to the existence of monsters. Tell them how you feel about that.
You saved their life from a monster due to an unlikely chain of events. Tell them what.

moves.name: Always The Victim
moves.description: When another hunter uses protect someone to protect you, they mark experience. Whenever a monster captures you, you mark experience.

outcomes.general: Passive — being protected or captured marks XP for you (and XP for the protector).

moves.name: Oops!
moves.description: If you want to stumble across something important, tell the Keeper. You will find something important and useful, although not necessarily related to your immediate problems.

outcomes.general: Narrative — declare you stumble onto something; the Keeper provides something important and useful.

moves.name: Let's Get Out Of Here!
moves.description: If you can protect someone by telling them what to do, or by leading them out, roll +Charm instead of +Tough.

outcomes.general: Passive — roll +Charm instead of +Tough for protect someone when leading or directing someone to safety.

moves.name: Panic Button
moves.description: When you need to escape, name the route you'll try and roll +Sharp.

outcomes.10_plus: You're out of danger, no problem.
outcomes.7_9: You can go or stay, but if you go it's going to cost you (you leave something behind or something comes with you).
outcomes.miss: You are caught halfway out.

moves.name: The Power of Heart
moves.description: When fighting a monster, if you help someone, don't roll +Cool. You automatically help as though you'd rolled a 10.

outcomes.general: Passive — help out during monster fights always counts as a 10+, no roll required.

moves.name: Trust Me
moves.description: When you tell a normal person the truth in order to protect them from danger, roll +Charm.

outcomes.10_plus: They'll do what you say they should, no questions asked.
outcomes.7_9: They do it, but the Keeper chooses one: they ask a hard question first; they stall and dither a while; they have a 'better' idea.
outcomes.miss: They're going to think you're crazy and maybe dangerous too.

moves.name: What Could Go Wrong?
moves.description: Whenever you charge into immediate danger without hedging your bets, hold 2.

outcomes.general: Passive — charging into danger holds 2; spend hold to: inflict +1 harm, reduce someone's harm by 1, or take +2 forward on act under pressure.

choices: Inflict +1 harm.
Reduce someone's harm suffered by 1.
Take +2 forward on an act under pressure roll.
moves.choices_note: Spend hold one-for-one on these effects.

moves.name: Don't Worry, I'll Check It Out
moves.description: Whenever you go off by yourself to check out somewhere (or something) scary, mark experience.

outcomes.general: Passive — going alone to investigate scary things always marks XP.

playbooks.moves_note: Pick three Mundane moves to start.

gear.note: Pick two mundane weapons and one means of transport.
mundane_weapons: Golf club, baseball bat, cricket bat, or hockey stick (2-harm hand innocuous messy)
Pocket knife or multitool (1-harm hand useful small)
Sledge-hammer or fire axe (3-harm hand messy)
transport: Fairly new car in decent condition

playbooks.luck_special: When you spend a Luck, you'll find something weird. Maybe even useful, but mostly just weird.
improvements: Get back one used Luck point
Get back one used Luck point
Take a move from another playbook
Take a move from another playbook
advanced_improvements: Get +1 to any rating, max +3
Change this hunter to a new type
Create a second hunter to play as well as this one
Mark two of the basic moves as advanced
Mark another two of the basic moves as advanced
Retire this hunter to safety
Get back one used Luck point.
Get back one used Luck point.

playbooks.name: The Professional
playbooks.description: It's kind of strange when your regular 9-to-5 job is to hunt down monsters. Still, that's the job I took on when I joined this outfit. It pays well, and the benefits are good. Like they say 'You don't have to be crazy to work here, but it sure helps!'
playbooks.experience_trigger: Whenever you roll and get a total of 6 or less, or when a move tells you to, mark experience.

agency.note: Define who you work for. Pick two resource tags and two red tape tags.

history_questions: Your relationship with them has romantic potential. So far it hasn't gone further.
They're on the Agency's watch list, and you've been keeping an eye on them.
You are related. Tell them how close.
You met on a mission and worked together unofficially. And successfully.
They've worked with the Agency before, and they're well regarded.
You were friends back in training, before the Agency recruited you. This could be military, law enforcement, or some weirder school: decide the details between you.
They pulled you (and maybe your team) out of a terrible FUBARed mission.
You got sent to 'deal with them' as a hazard to the Agency's policies one time. Tell them how you resolved this.

moves.name: Deal with the Agency (Mandatory)
moves.description: When you deal with the Agency, requesting help or gear, or making excuses for a failure, roll +Sharp.

outcomes.10_plus: You're good — your request for gear or personnel is okayed, or your slip-up goes unnoticed.
outcomes.7_9: Things aren't so great. You might get chewed out, but you get what you need for the job.
outcomes.miss: You screwed up: suspended or under investigation, or just in the doghouse. No help until you sort it out.

moves.name: Bottle It Up
moves.description: If you want, you can take up to +3 bonus when you act under pressure. For each +1 you use, the Keeper holds 1. That hold can be spent later — one for one — to give you -1 on any move except act under pressure.

outcomes.general: Risky passive — take up to +3 on act under pressure; each +1 gives the Keeper a -1 hold against you on future rolls.

moves.name: Unfazeable
moves.description: Take +1 Cool (max +3).

outcomes.general: Passive — +1 Cool (max +3).

moves.name: Battlefield Awareness
moves.description: You always know what's happening around you, and what to watch out for. Take +1 armour (max 2-armour) on top of whatever you get from your gear.

outcomes.general: Passive — +1 armour on top of gear armour (max 2-armour total).

moves.name: Leave No One Behind
moves.description: In combat, when you help someone escape, roll +Sharp.

outcomes.10_plus: You get them out clean.
outcomes.7_9: You can either get them out or suffer no harm, you choose.
outcomes.miss: You fail to get them out and you've attracted hostile attention.

moves.name: Tactical Genius
moves.description: When you read a bad situation, you may roll +Cool instead of +Sharp.

outcomes.general: Passive — roll +Cool instead of +Sharp for read a bad situation.

moves.name: Medic
moves.description: You have a full first aid kit, and the training to heal people. When you do first aid, roll +Cool. This move takes the place of regular first aid.

outcomes.10_plus: The patient is stabilized and healed of 2 harm.
outcomes.7_9: Choose one: heal 2 harm or stabilize the injury.
outcomes.miss: You cause an extra 1 harm.

moves.name: Mobility
moves.description: You have a truck, van, or car built for monster hunting. Choose two good things and one bad thing about it. Good things: roomy, surveillance gear, fast, stealthy, intimidating, classic, medical kit, sleeping space, toolkit, concealed weapons, anonymous, armoured (+1 armour inside), tough, monster cage. Bad things: loud, obvious, temperamental, beaten-up, gas-guzzler, uncomfortable, slow, old.

outcomes.general: Passive — you have a specialised monster-hunting vehicle with customised traits.

playbooks.moves_note: Deal with the Agency is mandatory. Pick three more from the remaining list.

gear.note: Pick one serious weapon and two normal weapons. Take either a flak vest (1-armour hidden) or combat armour (2-armour heavy).
serious_weapons: Assault rifle (3-harm far area loud reload)
Grenade launcher (4-harm far area messy loud reload)
Submachine gun (3-harm close area loud reload)

playbooks.luck_special: When you spend a Luck, your next mission from the Agency comes with lots of Red Tape. Lots.
improvements: Add a new resource tag for your Agency or change a red tape tag
Get command of an Agency team of monster hunters
Take a move from another playbook
Take a move from another playbook
advanced_improvements: Get +1 to any rating, max +3
Change this hunter to a new type
Create a second hunter to play as well as this one
Mark two of the basic moves as advanced
Mark another two of the basic moves as advanced
Retire this hunter to safety
Get some or all of the other players' hunters hired by your agency. They get the deal with the agency move, as well as salary and benefits.
Get back one used Luck point.

playbooks.name: The Snoop
playbooks.description: Cryptids have kept out of sight, but I'm going to find them and record them. The evidence will be incontrovertible, and I'll be the one who did it. I'm going to be a superstar one day, you mark my words! Hey... did you just feel a chill?
playbooks.experience_trigger: Whenever you roll and get a total of 6 or less, or when a move tells you to, mark an experience box.

crew.note: Decide if your crew is the other hunters, or if you have an entourage of up to three people. Assign each a job: camera, sound, editing, dogsbody, researcher, driver, director, producer, bodyguard.

history_questions: You're best friends from school/university (film class, maybe?)
You met this hunter when they killed a monster you were doing a story on. Ask them what it was.
This hunter almost defeated a monster but you did something to prevent that. Ask them what the monster was, then tell them what you did. Was it an accident or on purpose?
This hunter was in danger of having some unhelpful publicity, but you got the story killed.
You have a crush on this hunter. Ask them if they've noticed.
This hunter saved your ass when you did something stupid. Ask them what you did.
This hunter is a huge fan of your work. Ask them if they keep quiet about it or if everyone knows?
This hunter is a relation, close or distant. Decide between you exactly what.

moves.name: What Does That Feel Like?
moves.description: When you put your camera or microphone right in a person's face, they break off whatever they are doing. They might go right back to it after they deal with you, but you'll create enough time for the other hunters to act in the meantime.

outcomes.general: Narrative — your camera interrupts someone's action, buying the team time to act.

moves.name: Minor Celebrity
moves.description: At the beginning of each mystery, roll +Weird. On 10+, hold 2. On 7-9, hold 1. Spend hold to have someone you meet know of you in a positive light.

outcomes.10_plus: Hold 2.
outcomes.7_9: Hold 1.
outcomes.miss: No hold.

choices: Have someone you meet know of you in a positive light (autographs, hot leads, etc).
moves.choices_note: Spend hold one-for-one to be recognised positively.

moves.name: We'll Fix It In Post
moves.description: You can use anything you could conceivably have recorded as evidence for investigate a mystery, allowing you to check previous interviews, attack sites, and so on from the comfort of your laptop.

outcomes.general: Passive — recorded footage and audio count as evidence for investigate a mystery.

moves.name: Press Accreditation
moves.description: When you investigate a mystery by talking to witnesses, interviewing locals, or anything else requiring interpersonal skills, roll +Charm instead of +Sharp.

outcomes.general: Passive — roll +Charm instead of +Sharp for investigate a mystery when using interpersonal approaches.

moves.name: Truthiness
moves.description: Whatever you tell a normal person, they'll accept that you think it's true. If it's far out, they might think you're deluded, but they won't think you're lying.

outcomes.general: Passive — normal people always believe you believe what you say, even if they think you're wrong.

moves.name: The Mojo Wire
moves.description: When you spend a while reading all the latest news feeds looking for the weird stuff, take +1 forward.

outcomes.general: Passive — scanning news feeds for anomalies gives +1 forward.

moves.name: Relaxed Producer
moves.description: You're employed, with a regular pay check and little or no oversight. As long as you send in a story every few days, no matter how bizarre, you're set. Every now and again they'll send you somewhere in particular, and when that happens it usually involves supernatural activity.

outcomes.general: Passive — you have an employer who sends you (occasionally) toward supernatural events.

playbooks.moves_note: Pick three Snoop moves to start.

gear.note: You get a laptop, three recording devices, two detectors and one subtle weapon.

playbooks.luck_special: Whenever you spend a Luck point, you're going to have technical difficulties. Breakdowns, communication problems, weird noises in the recordings, etc.
improvements: Gain an ally: one of your existing crew members or the whole crew as an ally team
Get a haven, like the Expert has, with one option plus a film lab and editing suite
Take a move from another playbook
Take a move from another playbook
advanced_improvements: Get +1 to any rating, max +3
Change this hunter to a new type
Create a second hunter to play as well as this one
Mark two of the basic moves as advanced
Mark another two of the basic moves as advanced
Retire this hunter to safety
Get back one used Luck point
Make it big. You're a superstar now!

playbooks.name: The Spell-Slinger
playbooks.description: Fight fire with fire magic.
playbooks.experience_trigger: Whenever you roll and get a total of 6 or less, or when a move tells you to, mark an experience box.

combat_magic.note: Pick three options from the list. Your combat spells can combine any of your base spells with any of your effects. When using combat spells to kick some ass, roll +Weird instead of +Tough.
bases: Ball: 1-harm magical area close obvious loud
Wall: 1-harm magical barrier close 1-armour obvious loud
effects: Fire: Add '+2 harm fire' to a base. On 10+ the fire won't spread.
Force or Wind: Add '+1 harm forceful' to a base, or '+1 armour' to a wall.
Lightning or Entropy: Add '+1 harm messy' to a base.
Frost or Ice: Add '-1 harm +2 armour' to a wall, or '+1 harm restraining' to other bases.
Earth: Add 'forceful restraining' to a base.

tools_and_techniques.note: Cross off one of these — you don't need that one. You'll need the rest.

options.name: Consumables
options.description: You need certain supplies on hand to cast. They're used up when cast. Without them, take 1-harm ignore-armour when you cast.

options.name: Foci
options.description: You need wands, staves, and other obvious props. Without them, combat magic does 1 less harm.

options.name: Gestures
options.description: You must wave your hands obviously to use combat magic. If restrained, you can still cast but take -1 ongoing to combat magic.

options.name: Incantations
options.description: You must speak in an arcane language. If unable or unwilling to speak while casting, you must immediately act under pressure to avoid scrambling your thoughts.

history_questions: They act as your conscience when the power goes to your head. Ask them about the last time this happened.
Blood relation, though you were out of contact for years. Ask them how they reconnected with you.
Mentor from another life. Ask them what they taught you.
Your magic-fueled rescue of them introduced them to the supernatural. Tell them what creature was after them.
An old rivalry has turned into a tight friendship. Tell them what you once fought over.
You thought they were dead, and now they're back. What 'killed' them?
They're an on-again/off-again love interest. Ask them what keeps you apart. Tell them what keeps you together.
A comrade-in-arms. You've faced the biggest threats together.

moves.name: Tools and Techniques (Mandatory)
moves.description: In order to use your combat magic effectively, you rely on a collection of tools and techniques. Cross off one — you don't need that one. You'll need the rest. See the tools_and_techniques field for the four options.

outcomes.general: Structural — defines three requirements for your combat magic. Missing one imposes a specific penalty.

moves.name: Advanced Arcane Training
moves.description: If you have two of your three Tools and Techniques at the ready, you may ignore the third one.

outcomes.general: Passive — having 2 of 3 tools negates the missing one's penalty.

moves.name: Arcane Reputation
moves.description: Pick three big organisations or groups in the supernatural community. They've heard of you and respect your power. With affected humans, take +1 forward when you manipulate them. You may manipulate affected monsters as if they were human, with no bonus.

outcomes.general: Passive — three chosen groups know your name; +1 forward to manipulate affected humans; monsters manipulated as humans.

moves.name: Could've Been Worse
moves.description: When you miss a use magic roll you can choose one alternative instead of losing control: Fizzle (preparations ruined, start over with double prep time), or This Is Gonna Suck (effect happens but all glitches trigger except one of your choice).

outcomes.general: Passive — on a use magic miss, substitute a chosen bad outcome instead of losing control.

moves.name: Enchanted Clothing
moves.description: Pick an article of every-day clothing — it's enchanted without any change in appearance. Take -1 harm from any source that tries to get at you through the garment.

outcomes.general: Passive — one piece of clothing reduces harm that passes through it by 1.

moves.name: Forensic Divination
moves.description: When you successfully investigate a mystery, you may ask 'What magic was done here?' as a free extra question.

outcomes.general: Passive — on any successful investigate a mystery, you get a free 'What magic was done here?' question.

moves.name: Go Big or Go Home
moves.description: When you must use magic as a requirement for Big Magic, take +1 ongoing to those use magic rolls.

outcomes.general: Passive — +1 ongoing to use magic rolls when they are a Big Magic requirement.

moves.name: Not My Fault
moves.description: +1 to act under pressure when you are dealing with the consequences of your own spellcasting.

outcomes.general: Passive — +1 to act under pressure when dealing with your own spell consequences.

moves.name: Practitioner
moves.description: Choose two effects available to you under use magic. Take +1 to use magic whenever you choose one of those effects.

outcomes.general: Passive — pick two use magic effects; +1 to use magic when using either of them.

moves.name: Shield Spell
moves.description: When you protect someone, gain 2-armour against any harm that is transferred to you. This doesn't stack with your other armour, if any.

outcomes.general: Passive — 2-armour against harm transferred to you when you protect someone.

moves.name: Third Eye
moves.description: When you read a bad situation, you can open up your third eye for a moment to take in extra information. Take +1 hold on any result of 7 or more, plus you can see invisible things. On a miss, you may still get 1 hold, but you're exposed to supernatural danger.

outcomes.general: Passive — on 7+ read a bad situation: +1 hold and see invisible things. On miss: possibly 1 hold but exposed to supernatural danger.

playbooks.moves_note: Tools and Techniques is mandatory. Pick three more from the remaining list.

gear.note: You don't need much aside from any foci or consumables. Pick one backup weapon.

playbooks.luck_special: When you spend a Luck, the official council of wizards is going to poke their nose into your business. There is always some rule, tradition, or law you have bent or broken.
improvements: Take a move from another playbook
Take a move from another playbook

Change this hunter to a new type
Create a second hunter to play as well as this one
