playbooks.name: The Spooktacular
playbooks.description: I've traveled all over, pretty much everywhere you can make a dollar. I've made people happy, and I guess annoyed a few. But everywhere you go has monsters — this show included.
playbooks.experience_trigger: Whenever you roll and get a total of 6 or less, or when a move tells you to, mark an experience box.

the_show.note: Pick your show's specialty. This determines an ability from your time with the traveling show.

options.name: An Infernal Power
options.description: A Big Bad operated the Show for evil purposes. You signed the contract — take a three-box infernal favour track. Spend these as if they were Luck points. The Big Bad may restore these points when they wish, but first you must do something unforgivably terrible.

options.name: Magic & Illusions
options.description: You can cast confounding spells. When you weave an illusion, roll +Weird. 10+: everyone is fooled, led in a direction of your choice or you escape undetected. 7-9: misdirect or escape as 10+, but someone isn't fooled, or you gain unwelcome attention. Miss: it goes badly as magic always does.

options.name: Making Money
options.description: You've got practice bamboozling folks. When you mess with someone, roll +Charm. 10+: they don't know what's going on and overlook something of your choice. 7-9: they overlook something but they're more angry than baffled. Miss: you better run.

options.name: Problem Solvers
options.description: When you chat to someone or observe them for a few minutes, the Keeper will tell you what their biggest problem is right now.

options.name: Supernatural Creatures
options.description: You're not entirely human yourself. Pick a single supernatural move from any hunter playbook.

history_questions: This hunter worked the Show with you. Ask them for how long — it was at least a few months, time enough to become friends.
This hunter knew you as a kid. Ask what town you both hail from. Did you run away to join the Show?
'No, I certainly did not rip you off in that town a few years back. Must have been a different show, mate.'
This hunter was chasing a monster when the Show was in town, and thought it might be one of the crew. Was it? How did you resolve things?
This hunter is an estranged family member. Decide the exact relationship together.
Something got loose from the Show, a few years back, and this hunter ended up helping you secure it again. Tell them what got loose, then ask how they ended up hunting it too.
A fortune teller told you this hunter is part of your destiny. Ask what the rest of the fortune said.
This weird old person pointed this hunter out to you when you were in a bad spot. Ask them why they helped you get back on track.

moves.name: Put On A Show
moves.description: You can entertain people so they feel joy and forget their troubles. Say what you do and roll +Charm if it's pure performance or +Weird if you add a pinch of magic.

outcomes.10_plus: The audience is transported and happy. Troubles and trauma are eased in their minds, for a good while.
outcomes.7_9: Pick one: the effect just lasts a little while, or the regular time but there's a ruckus when you're done.
outcomes.miss: Something goes wrong with the performance.

moves.name: A Negligible Price
moves.description: You can make a magical deal to fulfill a desire for someone else. The price is that they reveal to you a secret — and then the world arranges itself to fulfill their desire, fitting the scale of the secret.

outcomes.general: Narrative — magical deal: they give you a secret, the world fulfills their desire proportional to the secret's size.

moves.name: Easygoin'
moves.description: You have the gift of being friendly and easy to chat to. When you try to make a good impression on someone you just met, roll +Charm.

outcomes.10_plus: They treat you as a trustworthy and friendly acquaintance.
outcomes.7_9: They open up more than usual but still regard you as a stranger.
outcomes.miss: They think you're playing them, and they'll not trust you or your companions.

moves.name: Pay It Backward
moves.description: Give yourself or someone else an advantage on any roll: roll 3 dice and select the best 2 for your result. Whenever you do this, the Keeper gains 1 hold. That hold may be spent to give any hunter a disadvantage: roll 3 dice and select the worst 2 instead.

outcomes.general: Risky passive — roll 3 pick best 2 as an advantage; Keeper gains 1 hold to force roll 3 pick worst 2 on any hunter.

moves.name: The Old Crew
moves.description: You have an ally team of Show folks. They are great at setting up a scene, creating a distraction, or even intimidating folks.

outcomes.general: Passive — you have an ally team skilled at diversions, scene-setting, and intimidation.

moves.name: The Game Is Fixed
moves.description: When you use magic in a crowded and chaotic place (like a fairground), take +1 forward. You may also choose from special effects whenever you use magic in such a place.

outcomes.general: Passive — +1 forward to use magic in crowded/chaotic places; additional effect options available.

choices: Someone fails a skilled task.
You disappear unnoticed and untraceable in a crowded and chaotic scene.
Instantly set up or pack away a carnival stall or camp site.
Track someone.
Get a bunch of cash.
moves.choices_note: Extra use magic effects available in crowded/chaotic environments.

playbooks.moves_note: Pick two Spooktacular moves to start.

gear.note: You get one camp tool, one vehicle, one mystical item, and all the paraphernalia.
camp_tools: Pocket knife or multi-tool (1-harm hand useful small)
Sock full of coins (1-harm hand stun)
mystical_items: Ghost Shades — sunglasses that see into the spirit world
Really Big Plush Dog (Animated) — somehow came to life; kind of like a regular dog
Returning 100 — spend this $100 bill and it comes back in a day or so
Scamulet — heats up whenever someone's running a scam on you
Wizard Tent — worn and crappy outside, big and luxurious inside
gear.paraphernalia: Tickets to a show, snack vouchers, decks of cards, pairs of dice, magic trick props, camping gear, makeup, costume accessories.

playbooks.luck_special: When you spend a point of Luck, you're going to run into someone you met at the Show. Could be good, could be bad, who knows?
improvements: Take a move from another playbook
Take a move from another playbook
advanced_improvements: Get +1 to any rating, max +3
Change this hunter to a new type
Create a second hunter to play as well as this one
Mark two of the basic moves as advanced
Mark another two of the basic moves as advanced
Retire this hunter to safety
Get back one used Luck point
Get your own traveling show. Maybe you take over your old one, maybe you start anew.

playbooks.name: The Spooky
playbooks.description: I can do things, things that normal people can't. But there's a price — I haven't paid it in full, yet, but the bill's gonna come due soon. It's best I don't tell you any more. You get too close, you'll get hurt.
playbooks.experience_trigger: Whenever you roll and get a total of 6 or less, or when a move tells you to, mark an experience box.

dark_side.note: Pick three dark side tags. The Keeper can ask you to act on these; doing so marks experience. Refusing locks your powers until the end of the mystery. As you mark Luck, requests get nastier.

history_questions: They taught you to control your powers, to the extent that you can control them at all.
You are blood-kin. Decide together exactly what.
You are married, or romantically involved. Decide between you the exact relationship.
You're old friends, and trust each other completely.
You used your powers on them one time. Decide if it was for selfish reasons or not, and tell them if they found out about it.
You've known each other some time, but since your powers manifested, you keep them at a distance emotionally.
You hope they can help you control your powers.
They saw you use your powers for selfish or vindictive reasons. Ask them who the victim was, then tell them what you did.

moves.name: Telepathy
moves.description: You can read people's thoughts and put words in their mind. This can allow you to investigate a mystery or read a bad situation without needing to actually talk. You can also manipulate someone without speaking. You still roll moves as normal, except people will not expect the weirdness of your mental communication.

outcomes.general: Passive — read thoughts, plant words, and use social moves silently. Normal rolls apply.

moves.name: Hex
moves.description: When you cast a spell (with use magic), as well as the normal effects, you may pick from: the target contracts a disease; the target immediately suffers 2-harm magic ignore-armour; the target breaks something precious or important.

outcomes.general: Passive — on use magic, add one of three curse effects in addition to normal results.

choices: The target contracts a disease.
The target immediately suffers harm (2-harm magic ignore-armour).
The target breaks something precious or important.
moves.choices_note: Add one of these effects to any use magic cast.

moves.name: The Sight
moves.description: You can see the invisible, especially spirits and magical influences. You may communicate with (maybe even make deals with) the spirits you see, and they give you more opportunities to spot clues when you investigate a mystery.

outcomes.general: Passive — you see spirits and magical influences; may communicate or deal with them; +opportunities when investigating.

moves.name: Premonitions
moves.description: At the start of each mystery, roll +Weird.

outcomes.10_plus: You get a detailed vision of something bad that is yet to happen. You take +1 forward to prevent it, and mark experience if you stop it.
outcomes.7_9: You get clouded images of something bad yet to happen. Mark experience if you stop it.
outcomes.miss: You get a vision of something bad happening to you, and the Keeper holds 3 to spend as penalties to your rolls.

moves.name: Hunches
moves.description: When something bad is happening (or just about to happen) somewhere that you aren't, roll +Sharp.

outcomes.10_plus: You knew where you needed to go, just in time to get there.
outcomes.7_9: You get there late — in time to intervene, but not prevent it altogether.
outcomes.miss: You get there just in time to be in trouble yourself.

moves.name: Tune In
moves.description: You can attune your mind to a monster or minion. Roll +Weird.

outcomes.10_plus: Hold 3.
outcomes.7_9: Hold 1.
outcomes.miss: The monster becomes aware of you.

choices: Where is the creature right now?
What is it planning to do right now?
Who is it going to attack next?
Who does it regard as the biggest threat?
How can I attract its attention?
moves.choices_note: Spend hold one-for-one to ask the Keeper these questions. Gain +1 ongoing while acting on the answers.

moves.name: The Big Whammy
moves.description: You can use your powers to kick some ass: roll +Weird instead of +Tough. The attack has 2-harm close obvious ignore-armour. On a miss, you'll get a magical backlash.

outcomes.10_plus: Successful attack: 2-harm close obvious ignore-armour.
outcomes.7_9: Attack lands as on 10+.
outcomes.miss: Magical backlash.

moves.name: Jinx
moves.description: You can encourage coincidences to occur, the way you want. When you jinx a target, roll +Weird.

outcomes.10_plus: Hold 2.
outcomes.7_9: Hold 1.
outcomes.miss: The Keeper holds 2 over you to be used in the same way.

choices: Interfere with a hunter, giving them -1 forward.
Help a hunter, giving them +1 forward, by interfering with their enemy.
Interfere with what a monster, minion, or bystander is trying to do.

The target finds something you left for them.
The target loses something that you will soon find.

playbooks.moves_note: Pick three Spooky moves to start.

playbooks.name: The Wronged
playbooks.description: They took my loved ones. Back then I wasn't strong enough to fight, but I studied, trained, and now I'm ready to cleanse the world of their taint. I'll kill them all. That's all I have left.
playbooks.experience_trigger: Whenever you roll and get a total of 6 or less, or when a move tells you to, mark an experience box.

who_you_lost.note: Pick one or more people you lost. Name them.

what_did_it.note: With the Keeper's agreement, pick the monster breed responsible.

history_questions: They helped you at a critical point in your quest for revenge. Tell them what you needed help with.
They stood between you and what you needed to find out. Ask them why.
They also lost a friend or relative to these monsters. Ask them who it was.
Relations, close or distant. Tell them exactly what.
You saved their life, back when they were a pathetic newbie hunter. Ask them what you saved them from.
You respect their hard-earned knowledge, and often come to them for advice.
They showed you the ropes when you were learning how to fight.
They saw you absolutely lose it and go berserk. Tell them what the situation was, and ask them how much collateral damage you caused.

moves.name: I Know My Prey (Mandatory)
moves.description: You get +1 ongoing when knowingly investigating, pursuing or fighting the breed of monster that caused your loss.

outcomes.general: Passive — +1 ongoing against the specific monster breed responsible for your loss.

moves.name: Berserk
moves.description: No matter how much harm you take, you can always keep going until the current fight is over. During a fight, the Keeper may not use harm moves on you and you cannot die. When the fight ends, all harm takes effect as normal.

outcomes.general: Passive — during a fight you cannot be dropped by harm moves; all deferred harm applies when the fight ends.

moves.name: NEVER AGAIN
moves.description: In combat, you may choose to protect someone without rolling, as if you had rolled a 10+, but you may not choose to 'suffer little harm.'

outcomes.general: Passive — protect someone is automatic 10+ in combat, but you cannot take the 'suffer little harm' option.

moves.name: What Does Not Kill Me...
moves.description: If you have suffered harm in a fight, you gain +1 ongoing until the fight is over.

outcomes.general: Passive — +1 ongoing while you have any harm, lasting until the fight ends.

moves.name: Fervor
moves.description: When you manipulate someone, roll +Tough instead of +Charm.

outcomes.general: Passive — roll +Tough instead of +Charm for manipulate someone.

moves.name: Safety First
moves.description: You have jury-rigged extra protection into your gear, giving you +1 armour (maximum 2-armour).

outcomes.general: Passive — +1 armour from jury-rigged protection (maximum 2-armour total).

moves.name: DIY Surgery
moves.description: When you do quick and dirty first aid on someone (including yourself), roll +Cool.

outcomes.10_plus: Counts as normal first aid, plus stabilize the injury and heal 1 harm.
outcomes.7_9: Counts as normal first aid, plus one of these (your choice): stabilise but patient takes -1 forward; heal 1-harm and stabilise for now but it returns as 2-harm unstable later; heal 1-harm and stabilise but patient takes -1 ongoing until fixed properly.
outcomes.miss: Something goes wrong.

moves.name: Tools Matter
moves.description: With your signature weapon (see gear), you get +1 to kick some ass.

outcomes.general: Passive — +1 to kick some ass when using your signature weapon.

playbooks.moves_note: I Know My Prey is mandatory. Pick two more from the remaining list.

gear.note: Pick one signature weapon and two practical weapons. You have protective wear worth 1-armour. Optionally take a classic car, classic motorcycle, plain pickup, or plain van.
signature_weapons: Sawn-off shotgun (3-harm hand/close messy loud reload)
Huge sword or huge axe (3-harm hand messy heavy)
Specialist weapons for your specific prey (4-harm against them, 1-harm otherwise; tags by agreement with Keeper)
Chainsaw (3-harm hand messy unreliable loud heavy)
practical_weapons: Assault rifle (3-harm close area loud reload)
gear.armour: Protective wear (1-armour, suited to your look)

playbooks.name: The Celebrity
playbooks.description: Fame is its own kind of supernatural power. People see what they want to see — but that can work for you just as easily as against you.
playbooks.experience_trigger: Whenever you roll and get a total of 6 or less, or when a move tells you to, mark an experience box.
playbooks.luck_special: When you spend a point of Luck, your fame either opens a door (career opportunity) or creates a new threat (scandal, obsessive fan, rival).
history_questions: They're your biggest fan — or were, until all this monster-hunting business. Ask them what changed their mind about you.
You two have history: co-stars, collaborators, or rivals in the same industry. Decide which.
They once covered for you when a monster encounter almost went public. Ask what story they told.
You used your platform to help them with something once. Ask them what it was.
They know your real name, the one before all this. Ask them how.
You owe them a favour — a big one. Ask them what it is.
They've been keeping something about you from the press. Decide together what it is.
You saved them from a monster once and had to explain the whole world to them afterward.

moves.name: Fakelore
moves.description: Pick two areas of occult lore your public persona is associated with (ghost-hunting show, horror author, etc.). When you investigate a mystery related to those areas, you may ask one extra question even on a miss.
moves.choices_note: Pick two occult lore topics at character creation.

moves.name: Scream Queen
moves.description: When you use your fame and fear to terrify someone, roll +Charm. On 10+: they flee or freeze in terror. On 7–9: they're scared but hold their ground. Miss: they're angry instead.

outcomes.10_plus: They flee or freeze in terror.
outcomes.7_9: They're scared but hold their ground.
outcomes.miss: They're angry instead.

moves.name: The Price of Fame
moves.description: When you leverage your celebrity status to get what you need, roll +Charm. On 10+: you get it, no strings. On 7–9: you get it, but it costs you — bad press, a debt, or unwanted attention. Miss: someone uses your fame against you.

outcomes.10_plus: You get what you need, no strings.
outcomes.7_9: You get it, but it costs — bad press, a debt, or unwanted attention.
outcomes.miss: Someone uses your fame against you.

moves.name: I Have People
moves.description: You have an entourage that can handle mundane tasks. Once per session, direct them to gather information, create a distraction, or handle logistics without rolling.

outcomes.general: Once per session, direct your entourage to gather information, create a distraction, or handle logistics without rolling.

moves.name: I Do My Own Stunts
moves.description: When you attempt something physically dangerous that you've rehearsed or trained for, take +1 forward.

outcomes.general: When you attempt something physically dangerous that you've rehearsed or trained for, take +1 forward.

moves.name: Disarming
moves.description: When you charm or flirt to defuse a tense situation, roll +Charm. On 10+: the tension breaks entirely. On 7–9: things calm down for now, but the underlying problem remains. Miss: you make things worse.

outcomes.10_plus: The tension breaks entirely.
outcomes.7_9: Things calm down for now, but the underlying problem remains.
outcomes.miss: You make things worse.

moves.name: But I Play One on TV
moves.description: When you act as if you know what you're doing (even when you don't), roll +Charm. On 10+: people believe you and act accordingly. On 7–9: they believe you for now, until something challenges it. Miss: someone calls your bluff at the worst moment.

outcomes.10_plus: People believe you and act accordingly.
outcomes.7_9: They believe you for now, until something challenges it.
outcomes.miss: Someone calls your bluff at the worst moment.

moves.name: Acting, My Dear Boy
moves.description: When you adopt a convincing persona or disguise, roll +Charm. On 10+: you're completely convincing for as long as you choose. On 7–9: it holds unless someone looks closely. Miss: someone sees through you immediately.

outcomes.10_plus: You're completely convincing for as long as you choose.
outcomes.7_9: It holds unless someone looks closely.
outcomes.miss: Someone sees through you immediately.

playbooks.name: The Covenant
playbooks.description: You never hunt alone. Whether it's a partner, a team, or a network, your strength comes from the bonds you've built — and the responsibilities that come with them.
playbooks.experience_trigger: Whenever you roll and get a total of 6 or less, or when a move tells you to, mark an experience box.
playbooks.luck_special: When you spend a point of Luck, your ally needs your help — they're in danger, trouble, or have a problem only you can solve.
playbooks.ally_note: You start with an ally (Watson, Rolodex, Unit, or custom). This ally can help out, gather information, and act independently. Define their name, role, and what they can do.
history_questions: They're part of your covenant too. Ask them what role they play.
They were there when your covenant was first forged. Decide together what crisis brought you all together.
You once had to choose between your covenant and them. Ask them what you chose.
They know one of your allies personally. Decide together how.
You saved their life once without telling your ally. Ask them why you kept it quiet.
They introduced you to someone now central to your network.
You owe them for something your network couldn't handle alone.
They have skills your covenant lacks. Ask them how they fill the gap.

moves.name: Get Down!
moves.description: When you throw yourself in front of harm aimed at your ally or a bystander, you take the harm instead and mark experience.

outcomes.general: You take the harm instead and mark experience.

moves.name: Fast Friends
moves.description: When you use your covenant's connections to call in a favour, roll +Charm. On 10+: they help immediately, no strings. On 7–9: they help, but want something in return later. Miss: they can't help — or won't.

outcomes.10_plus: They help immediately, no strings.
outcomes.7_9: They help, but want something in return later.
outcomes.miss: They can't help — or won't.

moves.name: Smash Cut
moves.description: Once per session, you may narrate arriving somewhere or having already done something off-screen, as long as it's plausible given your covenant's resources.

outcomes.general: Once per session, narrate arriving somewhere or having already done something off-screen if plausible.

moves.name: The Geek in the Chair
moves.description: When your ally does research or gathers information for you, treat their result as a 10+ on investigate a mystery.

outcomes.general: When your ally does research or gathers information for you, treat the result as a 10+ on investigate a mystery.

moves.name: Acolyte
moves.description: Your ally is more capable than most. Give them one move from a hunter playbook. They can use it once per session.

outcomes.general: Give your ally one move from a hunter playbook. They can use it once per session.

moves.name: Who Said I Was Alone?
moves.description: When you reveal that your ally was present and helping all along, turn a 7–9 result into a 10+ for one move you just made.

outcomes.general: Reveal your ally was present. Turn a 7–9 into a 10+ for one move you just made.

moves.name: Opening Doors
moves.description: When you use your covenant's reputation to gain access to people, places, or information, roll +Sharp. On 10+: no complications. On 7–9: you get access but someone takes notice. Miss: doors close and complications arise.

outcomes.10_plus: Access with no complications.
outcomes.7_9: You get access but someone takes notice.
outcomes.miss: Doors close and complications arise.

playbooks.moves_note: Pick 2 moves from the 7 listed. You also start with your ally.
playbooks.special: Once per session, you can save an ally from death. They are injured badly but survive, and return in the next mystery.

playbooks.name: The Envoy
playbooks.description: You serve a higher power — a council, an ancient order, an alien civilisation. They have a plan. You're not sure you fully understand it. But you trust the mission, and you're starting to see why it matters.
playbooks.experience_trigger: Whenever you roll and get a total of 6 or less, or when a move tells you to, mark an experience box.
playbooks.luck_special: When you spend a point of Luck, your Overseers take an interest — either they scrutinise your recent actions or issue a new demand.

overseers.note: Define your Overseers — a higher power that guides you from a distance. Pick their Values (what they want achieved) and their Concerns (what they're wary of).

task.note: Pick your Task — how you serve your Overseers.

options.name: Guide
options.description: You steer events toward outcomes your Overseers want.

options.name: Herald
options.description: You carry messages and warnings between your Overseers and others.

options.name: Watcher
options.description: You observe and report. Your Overseers trust your assessments.

options.name: Witness
options.description: You record. What you observe becomes part of a larger record.

secret_wisdom.name: Secret Wisdom
secret_wisdom.description: At the start of each session, roll +Cool. On 10+: hold 3. On 7–9: hold 1. Spend hold for +1 forward on a move your Overseers' knowledge can aid.

history_questions: They've noticed you seem to know things before they happen. Ask what they've said about it.
Your Overseers have an interest in them. You haven't told them yet.
They helped you complete a task without knowing why it mattered. Ask what they did.
They know you serve someone — they just don't know who.
You bent your mission to help them once. Your Overseers don't know.
They remind you of someone from where you came from.
You've been given conflicting instructions about them. You're still figuring it out.
They asked you directly who you work for. Ask what answer you gave.

moves.name: Strangely Normal
moves.description: When you blend into a social situation that should be awkward for you, roll +Cool. On 10+: no one looks twice. On 7–9: you pass, but one person notices something's off. Miss: you stand out in exactly the wrong way.

outcomes.10_plus: No one looks twice.
outcomes.7_9: You pass, but one person notices something's off.
outcomes.miss: You stand out in exactly the wrong way.

moves.name: Too Much Has Been Lost
moves.description: Once per session, when you protect someone from serious harm, you succeed automatically — no roll required. Your Overseers notice.

outcomes.general: Once per session, protect someone from serious harm with no roll. Your Overseers notice.

moves.name: The Council Has Made a Decision
moves.description: When you invoke your Overseers' authority to compel action, roll +Cool. On 10+: they comply, no questions. On 7–9: they comply but want reassurance or answers. Miss: they push back, and you've revealed more than intended.

outcomes.10_plus: They comply, no questions.
outcomes.7_9: They comply but want reassurance or answers.
outcomes.miss: They push back, and you've revealed more than intended.

moves.name: Prime Directive
moves.description: When you act in direct service of your Overseers' Values, take +1 ongoing until you act against those Values.

outcomes.general: When acting in direct service of your Overseers' Values, take +1 ongoing until you act against them.

moves.name: Servant of the Secret Fire
moves.description: When you face something that threatens to overwhelm you, roll +Weird. On 10+: you hold fast and the threat falters. On 7–9: you hold, but at a cost. Miss: you are tested to your limits.

outcomes.10_plus: You hold fast and the threat falters.
outcomes.7_9: You hold, but at a cost.
outcomes.miss: You are tested to your limits.

moves.name: From a Certain Point of View
moves.description: When you reframe a situation using knowledge your Overseers have given you, roll +Sharp. On 10+: the reframe is compelling — others act on it. On 7–9: it's partially convincing. Miss: your perspective seems incomprehensible.

outcomes.10_plus: The reframe is compelling — others act on it.
outcomes.7_9: It's partially convincing.
outcomes.miss: Your perspective seems incomprehensible.

moves.name: No One Listens to Zathras
moves.description: When you give a warning that others ignore and it comes true, mark experience and take +1 forward on dealing with the consequences.

outcomes.general: When you give a warning others ignore and it comes true, mark experience and take +1 forward on dealing with the consequences.

playbooks.name: The Forged
playbooks.description: You are a weapon. Literally. You take a human form, but your true nature is a weapon of unnatural power — and your bearer is the one you trust most.
playbooks.experience_trigger: Whenever you roll and get a total of 6 or less, or when a move tells you to, mark an experience box.
playbooks.luck_special: When you spend a point of Luck, your weapon form's Flaw manifests — or a Burden from your past as a weapon comes to light.

dual_nature.note: You have two forms: human and weapon. Your bearer is another hunter (or an important NPC). When wielded, your bearer adds your Weird to harm.

options: Bound (returns to a specific place if abandoned)
Proud (refuses to be used for unworthy acts)
Cursed (user takes 1-harm each time they wield you)

history_questions: They know what you are. Ask how they found out.
They've wielded you before — maybe as a test, maybe in desperation. Ask what happened.
You were made to destroy something they care about. Ask if they know.
You chose them as your bearer above all others. Ask if they feel the weight of that.
They helped you find your human form. Ask what it cost them.
You have memories of a previous bearer they remind you of.
They've seen your weapon form and asked you something about it. Ask what.
You trust them with your true nature more than anyone. Ask what they've done to deserve it.

moves.name: Partner
moves.description: When your bearer wields you, they add your Weird to harm. When you and your bearer work in concert on any roll, you both get +1 forward.

outcomes.general: Bearer adds your Weird to harm. You and your bearer both get +1 forward when working in concert.

moves.name: Tactical Advice
moves.description: When you advise your bearer on their next action, roll +Sharp. On 10+: they take +2 forward. On 7–9: they take +1 forward. Miss: your advice leads them astray.

outcomes.10_plus: Bearer takes +2 forward.
outcomes.7_9: Bearer takes +1 forward.
outcomes.miss: Your advice leads them astray.

moves.name: My Outlet
moves.description: When your bearer uses you to express something they can't say in words — rage, grief, love — both of you heal 1-harm.

outcomes.general: When your bearer uses you to express something emotional, both of you heal 1-harm.

moves.name: To My Side
moves.description: When you call your bearer to you (or vice versa), you both move to each other's location. No roll needed, but the Keeper may add a complication.

outcomes.general: You and your bearer can call each other to your side. No roll, but the Keeper may add a complication.

moves.name: Ritual Use
moves.description: When someone uses you in a ritual or ceremony (not combat), roll +Weird. On 10+: the ritual is enhanced, pick two effects. On 7–9: pick one effect. Miss: something goes wrong with the ritual.

outcomes.10_plus: The ritual is enhanced, pick two effects.
outcomes.7_9: Pick one effect.
outcomes.miss: Something goes wrong with the ritual.

choices: Extra hold or bonus on a following roll
Banish or bind a supernatural entity
Reveal hidden information
Protect the ritual space

moves.name: Don't Worry About Me
moves.description: When you are damaged (taking harm in weapon form), you can absorb up to 3 harm meant for your bearer. You must be repaired afterward.

outcomes.general: Absorb up to 3 harm meant for your bearer. You must be repaired afterward.

moves.name: Pointing the Way
moves.description: You have a supernatural sense for what needs to be done. Once per session, you may tell the group one thing that the current mystery requires — the Keeper confirms if you're right.

outcomes.general: Once per session, identify one thing the current mystery requires. The Keeper confirms if you're right.

playbooks.name: The Host
playbooks.description: Something lives inside you. Not a demon — something older, stranger. It gives you power. It has its own needs. You've made a kind of peace with it. So far.
playbooks.experience_trigger: Whenever you roll and get a total of 6 or less, or when a move tells you to, mark an experience box.
playbooks.luck_special: When you spend a point of Luck, the balance between you and your symbiote is upset — it wants more than usual, or you feel it pulling toward something.

symbiosis.note: Define your symbiote — a being that shares your body. Pick 2 Benefits and 1 Downside.
benefits: Enhanced senses (+1 ongoing to investigate a mystery)
Accelerated healing (heal 1-harm at the start of each scene)
Combat adaptation (take +1 forward once per combat)
Supernatural communication (speak to entities others cannot)
Toxic defence (attackers who deal harm to you take 1-harm)
downsides: Hungry (needs specific sustenance daily or you take 1-harm)
Territorial (must act under pressure when near others of its kind)
Dominant (it speaks up at bad moments; Keeper may cause it to act)
Exposed (those with supernatural sight can sense it immediately)
Needy (it wants connection — you must spend time communing with it each session)

history_questions: They know about your symbiote. Ask how they found out.
Your symbiote is interested in them specifically. You don't know why yet.
They helped you survive when the symbiote first bonded with you.
You've had to hold back around them because of your symbiote's reaction.
They're one of the few who doesn't treat you differently since the bonding.
Your symbiote once spoke to them directly. Ask what it said.
They know what your symbiote wants. Ask if they've told you.
You saved them without knowing whether it was you or the symbiote acting.

moves.name: Defensive Adaptation
moves.description: Your symbiote protects you in extremis. Pick one form: armour (1-armour at all times), regeneration (heal 1-harm when you take harm on a 10+), or deflection (once per scene, redirect one instance of harm to something nearby).
choices: 1-armour at all times
Heal 1-harm when you take harm on a 10+
Once per scene, redirect one instance of harm to something nearby

moves.name: Mutual Survival
moves.description: When you and your symbiote cooperate fully, roll +Weird. On 10+: hold 3. On 7–9: hold 1. Spend hold for +1 forward on any roll. Miss: the symbiote takes over briefly.

outcomes.10_plus: Hold 3.
outcomes.7_9: Hold 1.
outcomes.miss: The symbiote takes over briefly.

moves.name: Balanced Neurochemistry
moves.description: You are immune to all mind-altering effects, supernatural fear, and possession attempts.

outcomes.general: Immune to mind-altering effects, supernatural fear, and possession attempts.

moves.name: Open Your Mind
moves.description: When you let your symbiote expand your senses, roll +Weird. On 10+: ask three questions from the investigate a mystery list. On 7–9: ask one. Miss: the symbiote's senses overwhelm you.

outcomes.10_plus: Ask three questions from investigate a mystery.
outcomes.7_9: Ask one question.
outcomes.miss: The symbiote's senses overwhelm you.

moves.name: Predator and Prey
moves.description: When you hunt something, your symbiote assists. Roll +Weird instead of +Sharp for investigate a mystery when tracking or hunting a specific target.

outcomes.general: Roll +Weird instead of +Sharp when investigating by tracking or hunting a target.

moves.name: Collaborative Effort
moves.description: Once per session, you and your symbiote act together perfectly. You may make any basic move as if you rolled a 10+, without rolling.

outcomes.general: Once per session, make any basic move as if you rolled a 10+.

moves.name: Separation Anxiety
moves.description: When you are separated from your symbiote (magically or physically), you both suffer 1-harm per scene. But when reunited, you each heal 2-harm.

outcomes.general: When separated, both suffer 1-harm per scene. When reunited, both heal 2-harm.

A talisman that calms the symbiote
A memento from before the bonding

playbooks.name: The Interface
playbooks.description: You're wired in. Tech is part of you — whether through surgery, mutation, or something stranger. You can talk to machines. Sometimes they talk back.
playbooks.experience_trigger: Whenever you roll and get a total of 6 or less, or when a move tells you to, mark an experience box.
playbooks.luck_special: When you spend a point of Luck, you gain an unexpected social connection (a grateful AI, a hacker contact) or discover incredible tech — but it comes with complications.

integration.note: You have tech integrated into your body. Pick 2 Upgrades and 2 Faults. Also pick your origin.

origin.name: Rebuilt
origin.description: You were injured and saved by experimental technology.

origin.name: Technopath
origin.description: You were born with an innate connection to machines that eventually became physical.

origin.name: Volunteer
origin.description: You chose this. You wanted the edge. Maybe you still think it was worth it.

upgrades: Neural link (direct mental interface with electronics)
Sensory array (enhanced sight, hearing, or other sense)
Internal processor (+1 Sharp for data analysis)
Physical augmentation (one limb is stronger or faster than human normal)
faults: You need regular maintenance (miss it: take 1-harm per session)
Others can sometimes detect your signals
Your integration causes social friction (Charm –1 in intimate situations)
Some supernatural entities are drawn to your tech

history_questions: They know about your integration. Ask what they've done to help you maintain it.
They were present when you first went under the knife (or whatever it was). Ask what they remember.
You've used your tech to help them without telling them how. Ask what they assumed.
They've had to patch you up when your tech failed at the worst moment.
You trust their instincts over your data sometimes. Ask if they know that.
They once asked you something about your tech that you couldn't answer honestly.
You discovered something about them through your systems that you've kept to yourself.
They've treated you as fully human from day one. Ask what that's meant to you.

moves.name: Technomancer
moves.description: When you interface directly with a machine or system, roll +Sharp. On 10+: you get full access and can change, copy, or destroy anything in the system. On 7–9: you get limited access — pick one thing you can do. Miss: the system locks you out and alerts someone.

outcomes.10_plus: Full access — change, copy, or destroy anything in the system.
outcomes.7_9: Limited access — pick one thing you can do.
outcomes.miss: System locks you out and alerts someone.

moves.name: Expert Troll
moves.description: When you harass, misinform, or destabilise someone through digital means, roll +Sharp. On 10+: they're seriously disrupted — pick two effects. On 7–9: pick one. Miss: they trace it back to you.

outcomes.10_plus: They're seriously disrupted — pick two effects.
outcomes.7_9: Pick one effect.
outcomes.miss: They trace it back to you.

choices: Their communications are cut off
Their reputation or finances take a hit
They're forced to change location
They reveal something private while panicking

moves.name: Keep Going and Going
moves.description: Your integration keeps you going past normal limits. When you would be taken out by harm, roll +Cool. On 10+: you stay in the fight at 1-harm. On 7–9: you stay functional but take a lasting effect. Miss: you go down, but your systems keep recording.

outcomes.10_plus: Stay in the fight at 1-harm.
outcomes.7_9: Stay functional but take a lasting effect.
outcomes.miss: Go down, but your systems keep recording.

moves.name: Take the Shot
moves.description: When you use your integrated targeting systems, roll +Sharp instead of +Tough for kick some ass with a ranged weapon.

outcomes.general: Roll +Sharp instead of +Tough when using a ranged weapon to kick some ass.

moves.name: Hack the Planet
moves.description: Once per session, you may declare that you've prepared access to any digital system the team might need. No roll required, but the Keeper may add a complication from your Faults.

outcomes.general: Once per session, declare prepared access to any digital system. No roll, but Keeper may invoke a Fault.

moves.name: Virus Whisperer
moves.description: When you encounter a supernatural entity that has infected or merged with technology, you can communicate with it. Roll +Weird. On 10+: it talks back and you get useful information. On 7–9: you make partial contact. Miss: it notices you and reacts badly.

outcomes.10_plus: It talks back and you get useful information.
outcomes.7_9: You make partial contact.
outcomes.miss: It notices you and reacts badly.

playbooks.moves_note: Pick 3 from the 6 moves listed.

internal_computer.note: Free. Your integrated computer counts as having access to any publicly available database and can process information faster than normal.

optional_armour.note: May take 1-armour (integrated subdermal plating) at cost of -1 Charm.

improvements: Add a new Upgrade to your integration
Take a move from another playbook
Take a move from another playbook
advanced_improvements: Get +1 to any rating, max +3
Change this hunter to a new playbook
Create a second hunter to play in addition to this one
Mark two of the basic moves as advanced
Mark another two of the basic moves as advanced
Retire this hunter to safety
Erase one used Luck mark from your playbook
Your integration becomes fully seamless: ignore all your Faults once per session

playbooks.name: The Visitor
playbooks.description: You're not from here. Earth is strange. The monsters are stranger. But somehow, this place has gotten under your skin — and now you can't quite leave.

stats.note: Extreme spreads — one stat at +3, compensated by a –2 elsewhere.

playbooks.experience_trigger: Whenever you roll and get a total of 6 or less, or when a move tells you to, mark an experience box.
playbooks.luck_special: When you spend a point of Luck, something from your home culture collides with your life here — a misunderstanding, an opportunity, or something that pulls you back.

expatriation.note: Define your home culture and your connection to Earth.

options: Stronger or weaker sense of individual versus group
Relationship to time that doesn't quite map to human experience

why_left: I was sent here on a mission
what_pulls_home: Family or people I left behind
Something I need to bring back

broken_vessel.note: You have a vessel — ship, portal device, or other means of travel — that is broken. Describe it. Until it's repaired, you're staying.

history_questions: They were the first person to be kind to you when you arrived. Ask what they did.
They figured out you're not from here. Ask how.
You've explained something about your home culture to them that they took surprisingly well.
You once asked them something about human behaviour that they found deeply funny.
They helped you with something your home culture couldn't have prepared you for.
You have a name for them in your language that you've never translated. Decide what it means.
They've seen your broken vessel. Ask what they think of it.
You made a promise about Earth to them. Ask what you promised.

moves.name: Something Strange
moves.description: When you do something that reveals your alien nature in a way humans find unsettling, roll +Weird. On 10+: they're awed or rattled enough to comply with what you want. On 7–9: they comply but are spooked. Miss: they're frightened and hostile.

outcomes.10_plus: They're awed or rattled enough to comply.
outcomes.7_9: They comply but are spooked.
outcomes.miss: They're frightened and hostile.

moves.name: Always Learning
moves.description: When you encounter a new type of monster or supernatural phenomenon, ask one question from the investigate a mystery list for free — no roll required.

outcomes.general: When encountering a new monster type or phenomenon, ask one investigate a mystery question for free.

moves.name: Being Neighbourly
moves.description: When you use the customs of your home culture to help or honour someone, roll +Charm. On 10+: they're genuinely moved and will help you in return. On 7–9: it's a bit odd but appreciated. Miss: it goes wrong in a cultural misunderstanding.

outcomes.10_plus: They're genuinely moved and will help you in return.
outcomes.7_9: It's a bit odd but appreciated.
outcomes.miss: Cultural misunderstanding.

moves.name: Different World, Different Rules
moves.description: When you apply the logic of your home world to a problem here, roll +Sharp. On 10+: it works brilliantly — ask two questions or take +2 forward. On 7–9: it partly works — ask one question or take +1 forward. Miss: it doesn't apply at all.

outcomes.10_plus: Ask two questions or take +2 forward.
outcomes.7_9: Ask one question or take +1 forward.
outcomes.miss: It doesn't apply at all.

moves.name: Taste of Home
moves.description: Once per session, when you share something from your home culture with someone, both of you heal 1-harm and they get +1 forward on the next roll you both make.

outcomes.general: Once per session, share something from home. Both heal 1-harm, both get +1 forward on your next shared roll.

moves.name: Alien Anatomy
moves.description: Your physiology is different. You suffer 1-harm less from any source (min 0), but medicine and healing that relies on human biology works half as well on you.

outcomes.general: Suffer 1-harm less from any source. Human medicine works half as well on you.

moves.name: Otherworldly Techniques
moves.description: When you use combat techniques from your home world, roll +Tough. On 10+: 2-harm and pick two extras. On 7–9: 2-harm and pick one extra. Miss: 1-harm and you're off-balance.

outcomes.10_plus: 2-harm and pick two extras.
outcomes.7_9: 2-harm and pick one extra.
outcomes.miss: 1-harm and you're off-balance.

choices: +1 harm
Disorient target
Avoid counterattack
Create an opening for an ally
